verses.text: Rebuke not an elder, but entreat him as a father; the women as mothers and sisters. Gentle dealing. Treat each with fitting respect.
Rebuke not an elder, but entreat him as a father; the women as mothers and sisters. Gentle dealing. Treat each with fitting respect.
Honour widows that are 'widows indeed'; let children 'requite their parents.' Family duty. Care for one's own is a sacred charge.
Honour widows that are 'widows indeed'; let children 'requite their parents.' Family duty. Care for one's own is a sacred charge.
The true widow trusts in God; 'she that liveth in pleasure is dead while she liveth.' Two ways. Pleasure-loving is a living death.
The true widow trusts in God; 'she that liveth in pleasure is dead while she liveth.' Two ways. Pleasure-loving is a living death.
The true widow trusts in God; 'she that liveth in pleasure is dead while she liveth.' Two ways. Pleasure-loving is a living death.
He that provideth not for his own 'hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel.' Solemn word. Neglect of family disgraces the faith.
Let a widow be enrolled if aged and 'well reported of for good works.' Worthy women. The church honours the faithful aged.
Let a widow be enrolled if aged and 'well reported of for good works.' Worthy women. The church honours the faithful aged.
Refuse younger widows, who grow idle, 'tattlers also and busybodies.' A caution. Idleness breeds gossip and harm.
Refuse younger widows, who grow idle, 'tattlers also and busybodies.' A caution. Idleness breeds gossip and harm.
Refuse younger widows, who grow idle, 'tattlers also and busybodies.' A caution. Idleness breeds gossip and harm.
Let the younger marry and guide the house; let believers relieve their own widows. Order in care. Each family bears its own burden.
Let the younger marry and guide the house; let believers relieve their own widows. Order in care. Each family bears its own burden.
Let the younger marry and guide the house; let believers relieve their own widows. Order in care. Each family bears its own burden.
Let elders that rule well have 'double honour'; 'the labourer is worthy of his reward.' Honour leaders. Faithful elders deserve support.
Let elders that rule well have 'double honour'; 'the labourer is worthy of his reward.' Honour leaders. Faithful elders deserve support.
Receive no accusation against an elder but by witnesses; rebuke open sin, that others fear. Just discipline. Fairness guards the church's order.
Receive no accusation against an elder but by witnesses; rebuke open sin, that others fear. Just discipline. Fairness guards the church's order.
Receive no accusation against an elder but by witnesses; rebuke open sin, that others fear. Just discipline. Fairness guards the church's order.
Lay hands suddenly on no man; 'keep thyself pure'; some sins are open, others hidden. Careful and pure. Ordain with caution and live in purity.
Lay hands suddenly on no man; 'keep thyself pure'; some sins are open, others hidden. Careful and pure. Ordain with caution and live in purity.
